company.name: TechYenthra Technologies
company.subTagline: We build cutting-edge software, AI-powered platforms, and enterprise solutions that transform businesses and accelerate digital growth.

about.title: Who We Are
about.subtitle: A Technology Powerhouse Built on Innovation
about.description: TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited is a next-generation IT services company headquartered in India. We bring together brilliant engineers, creative designers, and AI specialists to deliver world-class digital solutions. Our team thrives at the intersection of technology and business value.
highlights: Full-stack software development with modern architectures
AI-first approach to solving real business problems
Agile delivery with a focus on quality and speed
about.teamDescription: Our diverse team of 20+ engineers, designers, and AI researchers brings deep expertise across industries including FinTech, HealthTech, EdTech, and Retail.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.text: To empower businesses with innovative technology solutions that solve real-world problems, drive efficiency, and create measurable value. We strive to be the trusted technology partner that companies rely on for their most ambitious digital initiatives.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.text: To be a globally recognized technology company known for engineering excellence, AI innovation, and the transformative impact we create for our clients. We envision a world where every business, regardless of size, has access to enterprise-grade technology.

values.title: Innovation First
values.description: We continuously push the boundaries of what's possible with technology, embracing emerging tools and methodologies.

values.title: Client Success
values.description: Our success is measured by the success of our clients. Every decision we make is guided by the value it creates for them.

values.title: Engineering Excellence
values.description: We maintain the highest standards of code quality, system design, and technical craftsmanship in everything we build.

values.title: Integrity & Trust
values.description: We operate with complete transparency, honesty, and accountability in all our client and partner relationships.

services.title: Web Development
services.description: Scalable, responsive web applications built with React, Next.js, Node.js, and modern cloud architectures. From MVPs to enterprise platforms.

services.title: Mobile App Development
services.description: Cross-platform and native mobile apps for iOS and Android. Fast, intuitive, and built for scale with React Native and Flutter.

services.title: AI & Machine Learning
services.description: Custom AI models, NLP pipelines, computer vision solutions, and LLM integrations that give your business an intelligent edge.

services.title: Cloud Solutions
services.description: Cloud-native architecture, migration strategies, and managed infrastructure on AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud Platform.

services.title: Data Analytics & BI
services.description: Transform raw data into actionable business insights with custom dashboards, data pipelines, and predictive analytics.

services.title: Cybersecurity
services.description: Comprehensive security assessments, penetration testing, secure code reviews, and implementation of enterprise security frameworks.

services.title: Enterprise Software
services.description: Custom ERP, CRM, and business automation systems built to streamline operations, reduce costs, and improve productivity.

services.title: UI/UX Design
services.description: Human-centered design that creates delightful user experiences. From wireframes to production-ready design systems.

services.title: DevOps & CI/CD
services.description: End-to-end DevOps pipelines, infrastructure-as-code, and automated testing frameworks that accelerate delivery.

services.title: IoT Solutions
services.description: Connected device ecosystems with real-time data processing, edge computing, and smart automation for industrial and consumer applications.

services.title: AI Chatbots & Automation
services.description: Intelligent conversational AI, virtual assistants, and workflow automation powered by LLMs and custom NLP models.

services.title: Blockchain & Web3
services.description: Smart contracts, decentralized applications, NFT platforms, and enterprise blockchain solutions on Ethereum and Hyperledger.

services.title: Government IT Services
services.description: Technology solutions for government departments and public sector undertakings — e-governance platforms, digital India compliance, citizen portals, and GeM-ready software procurement.

products.name: CodeSense
products.description: Automatically analyse pull requests, detect bugs, enforce coding standards, and accelerate your team's velocity — integrated directly into GitHub & GitLab.

products.name: Jothisham
products.description: Full-featured astrology SaaS platform with AI-powered birth chart analysis, live one-on-one consultations, Kundali matching, and personalised daily horoscopes.
highlights: AI Vedic birth chart & Kundali analysis

projects.title: CodeSense – AI Code Reviewer
projects.description: An AI-powered code review platform that automatically analyses pull requests, detects bugs, suggests improvements, enforces coding standards, and generates detailed review reports using LLM technology — integrated directly into your GitHub/GitLab workflow.
features: Automated PR analysis with bug & vulnerability detection
AI-generated improvement suggestions with code diffs
Custom rules engine for project-specific standards

projects.title: Expense Tracker App
projects.description: A comprehensive personal and corporate expense management app with smart categorization, budget alerts, multi-currency support, and AI-driven spending insights.

projects.title: Enterprise ERP System
projects.description: A fully customized ERP solution for a mid-sized manufacturing company, integrating inventory, procurement, HR, payroll, and financial reporting in a single platform.

projects.title: AI Chatbot Platform
projects.description: Multi-channel intelligent chatbot infrastructure supporting WhatsApp, web, and mobile with context-aware conversations, CRM integration, and analytics dashboard.

projects.title: E-Commerce Marketplace
projects.description: A feature-rich multi-vendor e-commerce platform with real-time inventory, payment gateway integration, dynamic pricing engine, and seller analytics.

projects.title: Healthcare Analytics Dashboard
projects.description: A real-time clinical data analytics platform for hospitals providing patient outcome prediction, bed management, resource optimization, and compliance reporting.

projects.title: OTT Streaming Platform
projects.description: A full-scale OTT streaming platform with live TV, VOD library, multi-device support, subscription management, adaptive bitrate streaming, and a personalised AI recommendation engine.
features: Live TV channels + Video on Demand library
Multi-device apps: Web, Android, iOS, Smart TV
Subscription tiers with payment gateway integration
Content CMS for publishers & admin dashboard

projects.title: Online Travel Agent Platform
projects.description: A comprehensive B2C and B2B Online Travel Agent platform covering flight bookings, hotel reservations, holiday packages, bus and cab bookings with real-time inventory, dynamic pricing, and a white-label reseller portal.
features: Real-time flight & hotel search via GDS/Amadeus API
B2B white-label portal for travel agents

projects.title: Jothisham – Astrology Platform
projects.description: A full-featured astrology platform offering AI-powered birth chart analysis, personalized daily horoscopes, live one-on-one consultations with certified astrologers, Kundali matching, and predictive transit reports — all in one seamless app.
features: AI-generated Vedic birth chart & Kundali analysis

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited ('we', 'our', or 'us') is committed to protecting your personal information. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your data when you visit our website or engage our services.

sections.heading: Information We Collect
sections.content: We collect information you provide directly to us, such as your name, email address, phone number, company name, and project details when you contact us through our website forms or via phone. We may also automatically collect certain technical data including IP address, browser type, operating system, pages visited, and time spent on pages through standard web analytics tools.

sections.heading: How We Use Your Information
sections.content: We use the information we collect to: respond to your inquiries and provide the services you request; send you project updates, proposals, and communications related to your engagement with us; improve our website and service offerings; comply with applicable legal obligations; and send you marketing communications about our services where you have given consent or where we have a legitimate business interest.

sections.heading: Sharing of Information
sections.content: We do not sell, trade, or rent your personal information to third parties. We may share your information with trusted service providers who assist us in operating our website and conducting our business, subject to confidentiality agreements. We may disclose information if required by law, court order, or government regulation, or to protect our rights, property, or safety.

sections.heading: Data Retention
sections.content: We retain your personal data only for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes outlined in this policy, including for the duration of any contractual relationship and any legally required retention periods thereafter. When data is no longer required, it is securely deleted or anonymised.

sections.heading: Cookies & Tracking
sections.content: Our website may use cookies and similar tracking technologies to enhance your browsing experience, analyse site traffic, and personalise content. You can control cookie settings through your browser preferences. Disabling cookies may affect certain website functionality.

sections.heading: Data Security
sections.content: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal information against unauthorised access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. These measures include SSL encryption, access controls, and regular security assessments. However, no method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure.

sections.heading: Your Rights
sections.content: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to access, correct, update, or request deletion of your personal information. You may also have the right to object to processing, request data portability, or withdraw consent at any time. To exercise these rights, please contact us at the details below.

sections.heading: Third-Party Links
sections.content: Our website may contain links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for the privacy practices of those websites and encourage you to review their respective privacy policies.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
sections.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of significant changes by posting the new policy on this page with an updated revision date. Your continued use of our services after such changes constitutes acceptance of the revised policy.

sections.heading: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us by phone at +91 81051 77337 or by email at info@techyenthra.com.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.intro: Please read these Terms & Conditions carefully before engaging TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited (CIN: U62011TN2026PTC194186) ('Company', 'we', 'us', or 'our') for any services or using this website. By accessing our website or entering into a service agreement with us, you accept and agree to be bound by these terms.

sections.heading: Definitions
sections.content: 'Client' or 'You' refers to any individual, company, or organisation engaging TechYenthra Technologies for services or accessing this website. 'Services' means any software development, AI solutions, consulting, design, DevOps, or related IT services provided by us. 'SOW' means Statement of Work — a written document defining the scope, deliverables, timeline, and fees for a specific engagement. 'Deliverables' means any code, designs, documents, or outputs produced by us under an SOW. 'Confidential Information' means any non-public business, technical, or financial information shared between the parties.

sections.heading: Acceptance of Terms
sections.content: By accessing our website, submitting an enquiry, signing an SOW, or engaging our services in any form, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to these Terms & Conditions in their entirety. If you are accepting on behalf of an organisation, you represent that you have authority to bind that organisation. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our services or website.

sections.heading: Services
sections.content: TechYenthra Technologies provides custom software development, AI and machine learning solutions, mobile app development, cloud architecture, UI/UX design, DevOps, cybersecurity consulting, and related IT services. The specific scope, deliverables, timelines, milestones, and pricing for each engagement are defined in individual Statements of Work (SOW) or service agreements executed in writing between both parties. We reserve the right to decline any engagement at our sole discretion.

sections.heading: User Responsibilities
sections.content: As a client or website user, you agree to: (1) provide accurate, complete, and up-to-date information required for service delivery; (2) cooperate and respond promptly to requests for input, approvals, or feedback; (3) ensure that materials you provide (content, data, designs, third-party software) do not infringe any third-party rights; (4) maintain confidentiality of any access credentials provided to you; (5) not misuse our website, APIs, or any software products we operate. Failure to fulfil these responsibilities may impact delivery timelines and does not entitle you to refunds or damages.

sections.heading: Acceptable Use Policy
sections.content: You agree not to use our website or services to: (1) engage in any unlawful, fraudulent, or harmful activity; (2) transmit viruses, malware, or any code designed to disrupt or damage systems; (3) attempt to gain unauthorised access to our systems or those of our clients; (4) scrape, harvest, or collect data from our website without permission; (5) infringe intellectual property rights of any party; (6) send unsolicited communications (spam); (7) impersonate any person or entity; (8) violate any applicable Indian or international law or regulation. We reserve the right to suspend or terminate access for any violation of this policy.

sections.heading: Account Security
sections.content: Where we provide access credentials (logins, API keys, SSH keys) for project environments, staging servers, or software products, you are responsible for maintaining the security of those credentials. You must notify us immediately at info@techyenthra.com if you suspect any unauthorised access. We shall not be liable for any loss or damage arising from your failure to maintain adequate credential security.

sections.heading: Subscription & Billing
sections.content: For SaaS products operated by TechYenthra Technologies (including CodeSense and Jothisham), subscription fees are billed as specified in the applicable product pricing plan. For custom project engagements, payment terms, milestones, and schedules are defined in the SOW. Unless otherwise agreed: invoices are due within 15 days of issuance; late payments incur a charge of 1.5% per month on outstanding balances; we reserve the right to pause work or restrict access on unpaid accounts; all fees are exclusive of applicable taxes (GST) which will be charged as required by law.

sections.heading: Third-Party Services
sections.content: Our services may incorporate or recommend third-party tools, APIs, cloud providers, payment gateways, or software libraries. We are not responsible for the performance, availability, pricing, or terms of third-party services. Any third-party costs (cloud hosting, API usage, licensing fees) are the client's responsibility unless explicitly included in the SOW. We will notify you before integrating any significant third-party service into your project.

sections.heading: Data Protection & Privacy
sections.content: We process personal data in accordance with our Privacy Policy and the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDPA) of India. Where we process personal data on your behalf as a Data Processor, a separate Data Processing Agreement (DPA) may be executed. We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect personal data and will notify you of any data breach affecting your data without undue delay.

sections.heading: Confidentiality
sections.content: Both parties agree to maintain strict confidentiality of all Confidential Information received from the other party. This obligation shall survive termination of any engagement for a period of 3 years. Neither party shall disclose Confidential Information to any third party without prior written consent, except: (a) to employees or contractors with a need to know, bound by equivalent confidentiality obligations; or (b) as required by applicable law or court order, in which case the disclosing party shall provide prior written notice where legally permitted.

sections.heading: Intellectual Property
sections.content: Upon receipt of full payment, the client shall own all custom code, designs, and deliverables created specifically for their project as defined in the SOW. We retain ownership of: all pre-existing proprietary frameworks, tools, and methodologies; general-purpose code, libraries, or patterns developed independently. Open-source components included in deliverables remain subject to their respective licences, which we will document. We retain the right to reference the project in our portfolio unless you request confidentiality in writing.

sections.heading: Termination
sections.content: Either party may terminate a project engagement with 30 days' written notice. Upon termination: the client shall pay for all work completed and reasonable expenses incurred up to the termination date; work completed but not fully paid for remains the property of TechYenthra Technologies until full settlement; we will deliver all completed work products upon receipt of outstanding payment. We may terminate immediately and without notice for material breach, non-payment exceeding 30 days, or conduct that violates our Acceptable Use Policy.

sections.heading: Force Majeure
sections.content: Neither party shall be in breach of these terms or liable for delay in performing, or failure to perform, any obligations if such delay or failure results from events, circumstances, or causes beyond its reasonable control, including: acts of God, flood, fire, earthquake, epidemic or pandemic, war, terrorism, civil unrest, government action, power failure, or internet service disruption. The affected party shall notify the other as soon as reasonably possible and shall use all reasonable endeavours to mitigate the effect of the force majeure event.

sections.heading: Warranties & Representations
sections.content: We warrant that our services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards and best practices. We do not warrant that any software will be entirely error-free or uninterrupted. We commit to addressing material defects within the warranty period specified in the SOW (typically 30–90 days post-delivery). THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED 'AS IS' TO THE EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, AND WE DISCLAIM ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

sections.heading: Dispute Resolution
sections.content: In the event of a dispute, the parties shall first attempt to resolve it through good-faith negotiation within 30 days. If unresolved, the dispute shall be referred to mediation under the Mediation Act, 2023 of India before initiating formal legal proceedings. If mediation fails, disputes shall be resolved by the competent courts of Madurai, Tamil Nadu, India in accordance with Indian law.

sections.heading: Limitation of Liability
sections.content: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, our total aggregate liability for any claim arising out of or related to our services shall not exceed the total fees paid by you in the three months immediately preceding the claim. In no event shall we be liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, exemplary, or punitive damages, including loss of profit, data, business, or goodwill, even if advised of the possibility of such damages.

sections.heading: Severability
sections.content: If any provision of these Terms & Conditions is found by a court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, unlawful, or unenforceable, such provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable, or severed if modification is not possible, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

sections.heading: Contact Information
sections.content: For all enquiries, legal notices, or communications regarding these Terms & Conditions, please contact: TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited | Email: info@techyenthra.com | Phone: +91 81051 77337 | CIN: U62011TN2026PTC194186 | Madurai, Tamil Nadu, India.

refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.intro: This Refund Policy applies to all services and SaaS products offered by TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited (CIN: U62011TN2026PTC194186). We are committed to fair and transparent billing practices. Please read this policy carefully before engaging our services.

sections.heading: Custom Software Development
sections.content: For custom development projects governed by a Statement of Work (SOW): payments made for completed and approved milestones are non-refundable; if a project is cancelled by the client before completion, fees are due for all work completed to date plus any reasonable expenses incurred; if a project is cancelled by TechYenthra Technologies due to our own inability to deliver, a pro-rated refund for undelivered work will be issued within 15 business days.

sections.heading: Advance Payments & Retainers
sections.content: Advance payments or retainers are held against future work. If you cancel before work commences, we will refund the advance minus a 10% administrative fee. Once work has commenced, advances are applied against completed work and any unused portion may be refunded at our discretion based on the circumstances of cancellation.

sections.heading: SaaS Product Subscriptions
sections.content: For monthly subscriptions to our SaaS products (including CodeSense and Jothisham): you may cancel at any time and your subscription will remain active until the end of the current billing period; we do not provide pro-rated refunds for partial months; annual subscriptions may be eligible for a pro-rated refund for unused months if cancelled within the first 30 days; after 30 days, annual subscriptions are non-refundable.

sections.heading: Free Trials
sections.content: Where we offer free trial periods for SaaS products, no payment is taken during the trial. If you choose not to continue after the trial, simply do not activate a paid subscription. No refund request is necessary.

sections.heading: Defective Deliverables
sections.content: If a software deliverable contains material defects that prevent it from functioning as specified in the SOW, we will correct the defects within the warranty period (as defined in the SOW, typically 30–90 days post-delivery) at no additional charge. Defect correction is our preferred remedy. Refunds for defective work are considered only if we are unable to correct the defect within a reasonable time after written notice.

sections.heading: Duplicate Payments
sections.content: If you are charged more than once for the same service due to a technical error, please contact us at info@techyenthra.com within 7 days. Confirmed duplicate payments will be refunded in full within 10 business days.

sections.heading: Refund Process
sections.content: To request a refund, contact us at info@techyenthra.com with your project or order reference, the reason for your refund request, and any supporting documentation. We will acknowledge your request within 2 business days and provide a decision within 10 business days. Approved refunds will be processed to the original payment method within 15 business days.

sections.heading: Non-Refundable Items
sections.content: The following are non-refundable under all circumstances: third-party software licences, API fees, or cloud infrastructure costs procured on your behalf; domain registrations or SSL certificates; work completed and approved by the client; fees for consulting or advisory sessions already conducted; any custom work where the client has materially changed the project scope after commencement.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.content: For all refund requests or billing queries: Email: info@techyenthra.com | Phone: +91 81051 77337 | TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited, Madurai, Tamil Nadu, India.

acceptableUse.title: Acceptable Use Policy
acceptableUse.intro: This Acceptable Use Policy ('AUP') governs the use of all websites, services, APIs, software products, and platforms operated by TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited (CIN: U62011TN2026PTC194186). By using any of our services, you agree to comply with this policy.

sections.heading: Permitted Use
sections.content: You may use our services and platforms solely for lawful purposes and in accordance with these terms. Permitted use includes: accessing and using our software products for their intended business purposes; integrating our APIs within the usage limits of your subscription plan; sharing feedback, feature requests, or bug reports through authorised channels; accessing our website for information about our services.

sections.heading: Prohibited Activities
sections.content: You must not use our services to: (1) violate any applicable Indian or international law or regulation; (2) infringe the intellectual property rights of any person or organisation; (3) transmit, distribute, or store malware, viruses, ransomware, or any malicious code; (4) conduct phishing, social engineering, or identity theft attacks; (5) harvest, scrape, or collect data from our platforms without written permission; (6) send unsolicited bulk communications (spam) to any person; (7) engage in any fraudulent, deceptive, or misleading activity; (8) facilitate gambling, adult content, or any activity prohibited under Indian law.

sections.heading: System Integrity
sections.content: You must not: attempt to gain unauthorised access to any part of our systems, networks, or databases; conduct penetration testing on our infrastructure without prior written authorisation; interfere with the proper functioning of our services through excessive API calls, Denial of Service (DoS) attacks, or other disruptive means; reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any part of our software except where expressly permitted by law; probe, scan, or test the vulnerability of our systems without authorisation.

sections.heading: Content Standards
sections.content: Where our platforms allow user-generated content, all content must: be accurate and not misleading; not contain defamatory, abusive, threatening, or harassing material; not infringe any copyright, trademark, or other intellectual property right; not contain personal data of third parties without their consent; comply with all applicable laws. We reserve the right to remove any content that violates this policy without notice.

sections.heading: API & Integration Use
sections.content: Users of our APIs must: use APIs only within the rate limits and quotas specified in their subscription plan; not share API keys with unauthorised third parties; not use APIs to build competing products that replicate our core service offering; attribute our services where required by our API documentation; report any security vulnerabilities discovered in our APIs to info@techyenthra.com immediately.

sections.heading: Consequences of Violation
sections.content: Violation of this Acceptable Use Policy may result in: immediate suspension or termination of your account or access; forfeiture of any fees paid (without refund); civil legal action for damages; referral to law enforcement authorities where criminal activity is involved. We will investigate suspected violations and cooperate with law enforcement as required.

sections.heading: Reporting Violations
sections.content: If you become aware of any violation of this policy by any party, or if you discover a security vulnerability in our systems, please report it immediately to: Email: info@techyenthra.com | Phone: +91 81051 77337. For security vulnerability reports, please refer to our Responsible Disclosure Policy at www.techyenthra.com/disclosure.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.content: For questions about this Acceptable Use Policy: Email: info@techyenthra.com | Phone: +91 81051 77337 | TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited, Madurai, Tamil Nadu, India.

sla.title: Service Level Agreement (SLA)
sla.intro: This Service Level Agreement ('SLA') defines the service standards, response times, and uptime commitments that TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited (CIN: U62011TN2026PTC194186) provides for its managed services and SaaS platforms. This SLA applies where explicitly referenced in a client's Statement of Work or subscription agreement.

sections.heading: Service Availability
sections.content: For managed hosting and SaaS platforms operated by TechYenthra Technologies, we target the following uptime: Production environments: 99.5% uptime per calendar month (equivalent to approximately 3.6 hours of permitted downtime per month); Staging/Development environments: 95% uptime per calendar month. Uptime is calculated as: (Total minutes in month – Downtime minutes) / Total minutes in month × 100. Scheduled maintenance windows (communicated at least 48 hours in advance) are excluded from downtime calculations.

sections.heading: Incident Response Times
sections.content: We classify incidents by severity and respond within the following timeframes: Critical (P1) — Complete service outage or data loss risk: Initial response within 1 hour, resolution target within 4 hours; High (P2) — Major feature unavailable, significant performance degradation: Initial response within 4 hours, resolution target within 24 hours; Medium (P3) — Minor feature impacted, workaround available: Initial response within 1 business day, resolution target within 5 business days; Low (P4) — Cosmetic issues, minor bugs: Initial response within 2 business days, resolution target within 30 days. Response times apply during standard business hours (Mon–Fri, 9 AM–6 PM IST) unless a 24/7 support plan is included in the engagement.

sections.heading: Support Channels
sections.content: Support is available through the following channels: Email: info@techyenthra.com (primary support channel for all plans); Phone: +91 81051 77337 (for Critical and High priority incidents); Dedicated Slack/WhatsApp channel (for enterprise clients where included in SOW). All support requests should include: client name, project/product name, description of the issue, severity assessment, and steps to reproduce (for software bugs).

sections.heading: Planned Maintenance
sections.content: We will provide at least 48 hours advance notice for planned maintenance that may affect service availability. Maintenance is typically scheduled outside of peak business hours (Mon–Fri, 9 AM–6 PM IST) where possible. Emergency maintenance required to address critical security vulnerabilities may be performed without advance notice; however, clients will be informed as soon as practicable.

sections.heading: SLA Credits
sections.content: If we fail to meet the uptime targets in a given calendar month, clients on eligible plans may request an SLA credit as follows: 99.0%–99.5% uptime achieved: 5% credit on monthly fee; 95.0%–98.9% uptime achieved: 10% credit on monthly fee; Below 95.0% uptime: 25% credit on monthly fee. Credits must be requested in writing within 10 business days of the end of the affected month and are applied as credits to future invoices. Credits are the sole remedy for SLA breaches and do not entitle the client to cash refunds.

sections.heading: Exclusions
sections.content: The following are excluded from SLA coverage and uptime calculations: scheduled maintenance windows communicated in advance; outages caused by the client's own actions, misconfigurations, or failure to follow our guidance; third-party service failures (e.g., cloud provider outages, DNS failures, payment gateway downtime); force majeure events as defined in our Terms & Conditions; outages caused by client-imposed changes that override our recommendations; free trial or development environment availability.

sections.heading: Client Responsibilities
sections.content: To enable us to meet our SLA commitments, clients agree to: provide timely approvals and feedback when required; not make unauthorised changes to production infrastructure without notifying our team; maintain up-to-date payment to avoid service suspension; report incidents promptly through the designated support channels; ensure their own network infrastructure and end-user devices meet reasonable technical requirements.

sections.heading: Review & Amendments
sections.content: This SLA is reviewed annually. We may amend the SLA with 30 days written notice to clients on active engagements. Significant improvements to service levels may be implemented without notice. If an amendment materially reduces the level of service, clients on annual contracts may request a review of their agreement.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.content: For SLA queries, incident reports, or service credits: Email: info@techyenthra.com | Phone: +91 81051 77337 | TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited, Madurai, Tamil Nadu, India.

cookie.title: Cookie Policy
cookie.intro: This Cookie Policy explains how TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited ('we', 'our', or 'us') uses cookies and similar tracking technologies on www.techyenthra.com. By continuing to use our website, you consent to the use of cookies as described in this policy.

sections.heading: What Are Cookies
sections.content: Cookies are small text files placed on your device (computer, tablet, or mobile) when you visit a website. They are widely used to make websites work efficiently, remember your preferences, and provide information to website owners. Cookies do not contain personally identifiable information on their own, but they can be linked to personal information we hold about you.

sections.heading: Types of Cookies We Use
sections.content: We use the following categories of cookies: (1) Strictly Necessary Cookies – essential for the website to function correctly, such as security tokens for form submissions. You cannot opt out of these. (2) Performance & Analytics Cookies – help us understand how visitors interact with our website by collecting anonymous information about pages visited, time spent, and errors encountered. (3) Functional Cookies – remember your preferences and settings to provide a more personalised experience. (4) Security Cookies – used by reCAPTCHA Enterprise (Google) to detect and prevent spam and abusive form submissions. These cookies assess risk signals and cannot be disabled without affecting form functionality.

sections.heading: Third-Party Cookies
sections.content: Our website uses the following third-party services that may set their own cookies: (1) Google reCAPTCHA Enterprise – for spam protection on our contact form. Google's privacy policy applies: https://policies.google.com/privacy. (2) Google Analytics (if enabled) – for website traffic analysis. Data collected is anonymised and aggregated. (3) Vercel – our hosting provider may set performance and security cookies. These third parties have their own privacy and cookie policies, and we do not control the cookies they set.

sections.heading: How Long Cookies Last
sections.content: Cookies can be either 'session cookies' or 'persistent cookies'. Session cookies are temporary and are deleted when you close your browser. Persistent cookies remain on your device for a set period or until you delete them manually. Most cookies we use are session-based or expire within 30 days.

sections.heading: Managing & Disabling Cookies
sections.content: You can control and manage cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to: view cookies stored on your device, block all or specific cookies, delete cookies at any time. Please note that disabling certain cookies may affect the functionality of our website, including the contact form's spam protection. To manage cookies, refer to your browser's help documentation: Chrome: Settings → Privacy and Security → Cookies. Firefox: Options → Privacy & Security. Safari: Preferences → Privacy. Edge: Settings → Privacy, Search, and Services.

sections.heading: Cookie Consent
sections.content: When you first visit our website, you may be asked to consent to non-essential cookies. You can withdraw your consent at any time by adjusting your browser settings or clearing cookies. Strictly necessary cookies (including security cookies) do not require your consent as they are essential to providing the service you have requested.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
sections.content: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time to reflect changes in technology, legislation, or our data practices. We will notify you of significant changes by posting a prominent notice on our website. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.

sections.heading: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions about our use of cookies or this Cookie Policy, please contact us at: Email: info@techyenthra.com | Phone: +91 81051 77337 | TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.

security.title: Security Practices
security.intro: TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited is committed to maintaining the highest standards of information security. This document outlines our security practices, the controls we have in place, and our commitment to protecting client data and systems.

sections.heading: Information Security Framework
sections.content: We follow industry-recognised information security frameworks and best practices. Our security posture is built on the principles of confidentiality, integrity, and availability. We conduct periodic risk assessments to identify, evaluate, and mitigate security threats across all our systems, processes, and software deliverables.

sections.heading: Secure Software Development
sections.content: Security is integrated into every stage of our software development lifecycle (SDLC). We follow OWASP Top 10 guidelines, conduct peer code reviews, perform static and dynamic application security testing (SAST/DAST), and validate all third-party dependencies for known vulnerabilities before use. All production code undergoes security review prior to deployment.

sections.heading: Access Control & Authentication
sections.content: Access to client systems, production environments, and sensitive data is governed by the principle of least privilege. We enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all internal systems, use role-based access control (RBAC), and conduct quarterly access reviews to revoke unnecessary permissions. All access is logged and monitored.

sections.heading: Data Encryption
sections.content: All data in transit is encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher. Sensitive data at rest is encrypted using AES-256 or equivalent standards. We do not store plaintext credentials, API keys, or secrets — all sensitive configuration is managed through secure vaults and environment variable management systems.

sections.heading: Infrastructure Security
sections.content: Our cloud infrastructure is deployed on reputable providers (AWS, Azure, GCP) with security groups, VPCs, and firewall rules limiting network exposure. We use automated vulnerability scanning on infrastructure components and apply security patches within defined SLA windows. Production environments are isolated from development and staging environments.

sections.heading: Incident Response
sections.content: We maintain a documented Incident Response Plan (IRP). In the event of a security incident, we follow a structured process: Detection → Containment → Eradication → Recovery → Post-Incident Review. Clients affected by a security incident involving their data or systems will be notified within 72 hours of confirmed detection, as required by applicable regulations.

sections.heading: Employee Security Training
sections.content: All employees and contractors undergo mandatory security awareness training upon onboarding and annually thereafter. Training covers phishing awareness, secure coding practices, password hygiene, data handling, and incident reporting procedures. Security knowledge is assessed through periodic tests.

sections.heading: Third-Party & Vendor Risk
sections.content: We assess the security posture of all third-party vendors and tools before integration into client projects. Critical vendors are reviewed for SOC 2 compliance, ISO 27001 certification, or equivalent. We maintain a list of approved tools and require vendors to comply with our data processing and security requirements.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.content: For security-related enquiries, please contact: Email: info@techyenthra.com | Phone: +91 81051 77337. To report a vulnerability, please refer to our Responsible Disclosure Policy.

dataProtection.title: Data Protection Statement
dataProtection.intro: TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited ('we', 'our', 'us') is committed to responsible and lawful handling of personal data. This Data Protection Statement explains how we comply with applicable data protection laws, including the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDPA) of India, and the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) where applicable.

sections.heading: Our Role
sections.content: Depending on the context, TechYenthra Technologies may act as a Data Controller (when we collect and use personal data for our own purposes, such as processing enquiries from our website) or as a Data Processor (when we process personal data on behalf of our clients as part of delivering software services). In both roles, we are committed to handling data lawfully, fairly, and transparently.

sections.heading: Legal Basis for Processing
sections.content: We process personal data only when we have a valid legal basis: (1) Consent — where you have given us explicit consent to process your data. (2) Contract — where processing is necessary to fulfil a contractual obligation. (3) Legitimate Interests — where we have a legitimate business interest that does not override your rights. (4) Legal Obligation — where we are required to process data to comply with applicable law.

sections.heading: Data We Collect and Process
sections.content: We collect and process: contact information (name, email, phone number, company); project and business information shared during engagement; technical data from website interactions (IP address, browser, page views); and communication records. We do not process special categories of sensitive personal data unless explicitly required and consented to.

sections.heading: Data Retention
sections.content: We retain personal data only for as long as necessary for the purpose it was collected, or as required by applicable law. Contact form data is retained for a maximum of 3 years unless a business relationship is established. Client project data is retained for the duration of the contract plus 2 years. Financial records are retained as required by Indian tax and accounting law (typically 7 years).

sections.heading: Data Subject Rights
sections.content: Under applicable data protection laws, you have the following rights: (1) Right to Access — request a copy of your personal data. (2) Right to Correction — request correction of inaccurate data. (3) Right to Erasure — request deletion of your data where lawful. (4) Right to Restrict Processing — request that we limit how we use your data. (5) Right to Data Portability — receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format. (6) Right to Object — object to processing based on legitimate interests. To exercise any of these rights, contact us at info@techyenthra.com.

sections.heading: Cross-Border Data Transfers
sections.content: Where personal data is transferred outside India or the European Economic Area (EEA), we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place, such as Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) or transfers to jurisdictions with adequate data protection laws. Our cloud infrastructure providers maintain data centres in approved regions and comply with applicable data transfer regulations.

sections.heading: Data Breach Notification
sections.content: In the event of a personal data breach, we will notify the relevant supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach, where required. Where the breach is likely to result in a high risk to individuals, we will also notify affected individuals without undue delay. We maintain a Data Breach Register and conduct post-incident reviews to prevent recurrence.

sections.heading: Data Protection Officer
sections.content: For all data protection queries, requests, or concerns, please contact our team at: Email: info@techyenthra.com | Phone: +91 81051 77337 | TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India. We aim to respond to all data protection requests within 30 days.

disclosure.title: Responsible Disclosure Policy
disclosure.intro: TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited values the security community's contributions to keeping our systems and our clients' systems secure. We encourage responsible disclosure of security vulnerabilities and commit to working collaboratively with researchers who report issues in good faith.

sections.heading: Scope
sections.content: This policy applies to security vulnerabilities discovered in: www.techyenthra.com and its subdomains; publicly accessible APIs operated by TechYenthra; and software products owned and operated by TechYenthra (including CodeSense and Jothisham). This policy does not apply to vulnerabilities in third-party products or client-owned systems, which should be reported directly to the respective vendor.

sections.heading: How to Report
sections.content: To report a vulnerability, please email us at info@techyenthra.com with the subject line 'Responsible Disclosure – [Brief Description]'. Your report should include: a clear description of the vulnerability; the affected URL, endpoint, or component; steps to reproduce the issue; the potential impact or severity; your contact information for follow-up. Please do not publicly disclose the vulnerability until we have had an opportunity to investigate and remediate it.

sections.heading: What We Ask of Researchers
sections.content: When conducting security research, please: (1) Act in good faith — do not exploit the vulnerability beyond what is necessary to demonstrate the issue. (2) Avoid accessing, modifying, or deleting data that does not belong to you. (3) Do not perform Denial of Service (DoS) attacks, social engineering, or physical attacks. (4) Do not access accounts or systems without explicit permission. (5) Keep findings confidential until we confirm remediation or agree on a coordinated disclosure date.

sections.heading: Our Commitments
sections.content: When you report a vulnerability in good faith and follow this policy, we commit to: acknowledging receipt of your report within 3 business days; providing an initial assessment of the report's validity and severity within 10 business days; keeping you informed of our remediation progress; notifying you when the issue is resolved; and not pursuing legal action against researchers who act in good faith in accordance with this policy.

sections.heading: Severity Classification
sections.content: We classify vulnerabilities using the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS): Critical (9.0–10.0) — remediated within 24 hours; High (7.0–8.9) — remediated within 7 days; Medium (4.0–6.9) — remediated within 30 days; Low (0.1–3.9) — remediated within 90 days. We prioritise fixes based on severity and potential impact on users and client data.

sections.heading: Recognition
sections.content: We are grateful to security researchers who help improve the security of our systems. While we do not currently offer a formal bug bounty programme, we acknowledge researchers who report valid vulnerabilities by crediting them in our security acknowledgements (with their permission) and expressing our sincere gratitude.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.content: Security reports: info@techyenthra.com (Subject: Responsible Disclosure – [Issue]) | Phone: +91 81051 77337. For non-security enquiries, please use our contact form at www.techyenthra.com/contact.

businessContinuity.title: Business Continuity Statement
businessContinuity.intro: TechYenthra Technologies Private Limited is committed to ensuring the continuity of our services and the protection of our clients' interests in the event of disruptive incidents. This statement outlines our approach to business continuity, disaster recovery, and operational resilience.

sections.heading: Our Commitment
sections.content: We recognise that disruptions to our operations — whether caused by technology failures, natural disasters, cyber incidents, or other unforeseen events — can significantly impact our clients. We are committed to maintaining robust business continuity plans (BCP) that minimise disruption, protect critical data, and ensure the fastest possible return to normal operations.

sections.heading: Business Impact Analysis
sections.content: We have conducted a Business Impact Analysis (BIA) to identify our critical business functions, key dependencies, and the maximum tolerable downtime for each. Critical functions include: client project delivery, code repository access, communication systems, cloud infrastructure management, and data backup systems. The BIA is reviewed and updated annually or following significant organisational changes.

sections.heading: Recovery Objectives
sections.content: Our recovery targets for critical systems are: Recovery Time Objective (RTO) — the maximum acceptable time to restore a critical service — is 4 hours for mission-critical systems and 24 hours for non-critical systems. Recovery Point Objective (RPO) — the maximum acceptable data loss — is 1 hour for critical client data and 24 hours for operational data. These targets are tested through periodic drills and simulations.

sections.heading: Data Backup & Recovery
sections.content: All critical data and code repositories are backed up automatically on a daily basis, with critical data backed up hourly. Backups are stored in geographically redundant locations using reputable cloud providers. Backup integrity is verified through automated restoration tests performed monthly. In the event of data loss, we follow a documented recovery procedure to restore data to the most recent clean backup.

sections.heading: Redundant Infrastructure
sections.content: Our cloud infrastructure is designed with redundancy at multiple levels: multi-availability-zone deployments, load-balanced application servers, replicated databases, and CDN-backed static assets. We monitor infrastructure health 24/7 using automated alerting and on-call escalation procedures to ensure rapid response to any system anomalies.

sections.heading: Incident Management
sections.content: When a disruptive event occurs, our Incident Response Team (IRT) is activated. The IRT follows a structured process: initial assessment and classification; immediate containment and mitigation; client communication (within 1 hour of confirmed disruption for affected clients); recovery execution; and post-incident review. A dedicated communication channel is established for each major incident to keep all stakeholders informed.

sections.heading: Remote Working Capability
sections.content: Our team is equipped and trained to operate fully remotely. All development tools, communication platforms, code repositories, and project management systems are cloud-based and accessible securely from any location. This ensures that physical disruptions to any single office location do not halt our operations.

sections.heading: Testing & Review
sections.content: We test our Business Continuity Plan through: annual tabletop exercises simulating major disruption scenarios; semi-annual backup restoration tests; quarterly review of contact lists and escalation procedures; and post-incident reviews following any real disruption. The BCP is formally reviewed and approved by management annually.

sections.heading: Client Communication
sections.content: In the event of a disruption affecting client services, we will proactively communicate: the nature and scope of the disruption; estimated time to resolution; interim workarounds where available; and regular status updates until full service is restored. Client enquiries during a disruption can be directed to info@techyenthra.com or +91 81051 77337.
